title: NOx_formation_in_turbulent_lean-premixed_combustion_with_minimum_heat_losses_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 1]
1. Mathematical equations

[NOx] = tau*(5.96 x 10^7)*exp(-31,500/Tg) (ppm)

2. Graphs/charts - NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3. Tables - NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4. Diagrams - NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5. Key numbers, constants, parameters
* Inlet temperature: 323 K
* Equivalence ratio: 0.5 to 1.0 for CH4/air mixtures and from 0.6 to 0.7 for H2/air mixtures
* Combustion gas temperature (Tg): 1390-2200 K
* Tg < 1700 K: NOx concentrations were independent of residence time
* Tg > 1700 K: NOx formation was contributed primarily by the thermal mechanism.

title: NOx_formation_in_turbulent_lean-premixed_combustion_with_minimum_heat_losses_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
**1. Mathematical equations:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 1: Schematic of impinging jet reactor**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Gas sampling
        *   Mixture (with arrows indicating flow)
        *   Thermocouple junction
        *   Spherical reactor
        *   Cubic chamber
        *   Exhaust gas
        *   Dimensions: L, 460, 230, phi20

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   Combustion gas temperatures: 1390-2200 K
*   Spherical ceramic reactor diameter: 150 mm
*   Ceramic tubes diameter: 20 mm
*   Number of holes on the reactor wall: 40
*   Hole diameter: 12 mm
*   Cubic chamber dimensions: 230 mm cube
*   CH4/air and H2/air mixture temperature: 323 K
*   Equivalence ratio (phi) for CH4/air: 0.5 to 1.0
*   Equivalence ratio (phi) for H2/air: 0.6 to 0.7
*   Flashback temperature for H2/air mixtures: > 1700 K
*   Reactor mean residence time (tau): 5.3 to 35.0 ms
*   Jet velocity (V): 20, 40, and 80 m/s
*   Distance between tube exits (L): 20, 40, and 150 mm
*   Mass flow rates (Qm): 13.8, 27.6, and 55.2 g/s
*   Thermocouple composition: Pt-Rh 20%/Pt-Rh 40%
*   Heat loss from reactor: < 8%
*   Combustion gas temperature difference from adiabatic: 50-110 K
*   Hot-wire probe diameter: 5 um
*   Gas sampling probe orifice diameter: 1 mm
*   Combustion temperature below which Fenimore and zeldovich mechanisms become small:1800 K

title: NOx_formation_in_turbulent_lean-premixed_combustion_with_minimum_heat_losses_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Okay, here's the requested technical content extracted from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   \[NOx] = tau * (5.96 x 10^7) * exp(-31,500/Tg) (ppm)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 2:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Distance L (mm)
        *   Y-axis: Maximum RMS Fluctuation of Velocity (m/s)
    *   Curves: Data points showing the relationship between distance L and maximum RMS fluctuation of velocity.
    *   Key value: V=20 m/s

*   **Figure 3:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: 10000/Tg (1/K)
        *   Y-axis: NOx Formation Rate (ppm/ms)
    *   Curves: Various data sets representing NOx formation rate as a function of inverse gas temperature (10000/Tg) for different turbulence levels and fuel mixtures.
    *   Key values: H₂/Air Premixed Combustion

**3. Tables:**

NO_TABLE_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Diagram within Figure 2:**
    *   Components: Two tubes separated by distance L.
    *   Labels:
        *   Tube
        *   L (separation distance)
        *   X (direction)
        *   Y (direction)
        *   Measurement Point

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   1.  25 (times tube diameter)
*   V = 20 m/s
*   Tg > 1700 K (10^4/Tg < 5.88)
*   Activation energy: 63 kcal/mol (based on the Arrhenius relationship), 55 kcal/mol
*   5.  96 x 10^7
*   31,500

I hope this is helpful! Let me know if you have other request.

title: NOx_formation_in_turbulent_lean-premixed_combustion_with_minimum_heat_losses_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical content extracted from the image you provided:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**  NO_x formation is proportional to its production time

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Axes:**

    *   Y-axis: NO\_x Formation Rate (ppm/ms)
    *   X-axis: 10000/T\_g (1/K)
*   **Curves:**
    *   One curve labelled 'this study' has values from 4.5-6.5 on the x axis and has NO\_x Formation Rate values from 10-0.1 ppm/ms
    *   One curve as an upwards-pointing triangle labelled 'C. J. Jachimowski /τ: 2.4-2.6ms [4]'
    *   One curve as a circular label labelled 'R.C. Steele /τ: 1.7-7.4ms [6]'
*   **Key Values:**  None explicitly defined apart from the curves shown

**3. Tables:**

*   **Headers:**

    *   T (K)
    *   τ (ms)
    *    NO\_x (ppm)
    *   CO (ppm)
    *   EI(NO\_x) (g/kg)

*   **Data:** (Representative Sample)
    *   T=1395.6, τ=8.22, NO\_x=1.8, CO=82, EI(NO\_x)=0.09
    *   T=1445.2, τ=7.82, NO\_x=1.9, CO=72, EI(NO\_x)=0.10
    *   T=1473.0, τ=7.44, NO\_x=1.6, CO=76, EI(NO\_x)=0.08
    *   T=1731.7, τ=6.35, NO\_x=5.5, CO=82, EI(NO\_x)=0.25
    *   T=1883.9, τ=6.10, NO\_x=17.6, CO=104, EI(NO\_x)=0.71

        *(Data continues similarly for various temperatures, residence times, and emission indices. Refer to full table for all values.)*
    * Column headers are repeating with separate blocks for CH4 fuels at V=80 m/s, V=40 m/s and V=20 m/s and with H2 fuel at V=20 m/s

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   V = 80 m/s
*   V = 40 m/s
*   V = 20 m/s
*   Activation energy range: 40-65 kcal/mol
*   Thermal NO formation in pure oxygen/nitrogen: 134 kcal/mol
*   Heat of formation of atomic oxygen: 59 kcal/mol

title: NOx_formation_in_turbulent_lean-premixed_combustion_with_minimum_heat_losses_
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   [NOx] = τ(5.96 x 10^7) exp(-31,500/Tg) (ppm)
    *   where τ is in ms and Tg is in K

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Axes:**
    *   y-axis: NOx (ppm) logarithmic scale from 0.1 to 1000
    *   x-axis: Tg (K) linear scale from 1400 to 1700
*   **Curves:**
    *   H2/Air premixed Combustion: Increasing linear trend
*   **Key Values:**
    *   τ (ms): 7.0-7.8, 13.0-15.4, 26.0-35.0
    *   L (mm): 150 40, 150 40, 150 20
    *   V (m/s): 80, 40, 20

**3. Tables:**

NO_TABLE_FOUND

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_DIAGRAM_FOUND

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   5.96 x 10^7 is a constant in the NOx equation
*   31,500 is a constant in the NOx equation
*   Tg < 1700 K condition.
*   Combustion gas temperatures of 1650-1850 K
